substep.title: Select the Risk Lenses
substep.action:
If categories were supplied in the dispatch, use them. Otherwise select the lenses that apply to this approach from:
- Security — injection, auth/authz gaps, data exposure, secrets handling, supply-chain. Applies to almost any code approach. - Performance — latency, throughput, scalability bottlenecks, resource exhaustion. Applies when scale or responsiveness matter. - Regulatory / Compliance — data residency, PII handling, licensing, standards. Applies only when the approach touches regulated data or licensing. - Operational — deployment, rollback, observability, failure recovery, on-call burden. Applies to almost any shipped change. - Correctness / Data-integrity — consistency, race conditions, data loss. Applies when state or concurrency is involved. - Cost — infrastructure spend, scaling cost. Applies when the approach changes resource consumption materially.
Select the lenses that genuinely apply; do not pad the register with categories the approach does not touch. Security and operational nearly always apply to code; regulatory rarely does.

substep.title: Scope Gate
substep.action:
A risk register assesses one approach. If "the approach" is actually a whole system or many independent changes, no single register would cohere. Emit to stdout and stop:
SCOPE_TOO_LARGE Reason: the approach spans multiple independent changes Suggested split:

check.template-output:
Present the selected risk categories and why each applies, before scanning.
[a] Adjust (add/drop a category) [c] Continue [y] YOLO
